title: About this archive
text:
A personal archive of Donald Trump’s public messaging — every tweet that survived the Trump Twitter Archive, every Truth Social post mirrored by the CNN feed, every speech and statement catalogued by the UCSB American Presidency Project — aggregated into one searchable collection.
The intent is curation, not commentary. No opinions are added. The only thing this site does beyond hosting the record is group posts into recurring topics so they’re easier to browse.

title: About this archive
text:
A personal archive of Donald Trump’s public messaging — every tweet that survived the Trump Twitter Archive, every Truth Social post mirrored by the CNN feed, every speech and statement catalogued by the UCSB American Presidency Project — aggregated into one searchable collection.

title: About this archive
text:
How topics were assigned
Each post was embedded with a small open-source language model (sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2, 384-dim). For each of eighteen hand-written “theme anchors” (a set of example sentences on that topic), we computed a centroid in embedding space. Each post gets the top three themes whose centroid is closest to its own embedding, above a cosine-similarity threshold.
Nicknames — Crooked Hillary, Sleepy Joe, Little Marco, Pocahontas, and about forty more — are matched with a curated regex list and always force inclusion of the Good / Bad Nicknames theme, regardless of what else the post is about.
All of this runs locally on a CPU, with no external API calls.

title: About this archive
text:
The “read aloud” voice
Where you see a Read aloud button, the clip is a voice clone of Donald Trump generated locally from a short reference sample of a public-domain speech. The audio is for this site only — not for redistribution, not downloadable in bulk, not for any commercial purpose. It exists so a reader can hear the posts in something approximating the speaker’s own cadence, which is part of how they came across originally.
If you’d rather it just use your browser’s built-in voice, click the small ⚙ in the footer to switch.

title: About this archive
text:
Known gaps

@POTUS45 and @WhiteHouse45 official-capacity accounts are archived by the National Archives but not available as bulk downloads. We pulled what the Internet Archive’s Wayback Machine had captured. About 19,000 more captures remain to hydrate in a future refresh.
Post-reinstatement @realDonaldTrump posts (November 2022 onward) are sparse and the public nitter mirrors we rely on were unreachable at build time. We’ll retry quarterly.
Rally transcripts pre-2015 are partial.

title: About this archive
text:
Refresh cadence
Quarterly. The build scripts are idempotent and upsert new records, so each refresh just pulls whatever is new from the mutable sources (Truth Social, any post-2022 Twitter, newly-archived speeches).

text:

Voice cloning → Coqui XTTS-v2 (MPL 2.0)

title: Donald Trump — In His Own Words
text:
Donald Trump — In His Own Words
Eighty-seven thousand posts across Twitter and Truth Social, more than eight hundred speeches and public statements, from 2009 to today. Curated into eighteen recurring topics. Read by year, by topic, or follow the path across the country.

  87,146posts

title: Donald Trump — In His Own Words
text:
A note for readers
These are Donald Trump’s words, not anyone else’s. Every post and every speech here comes from the public record — tweets captured by the Trump Twitter Archive, Truth Social posts mirrored in real time, speeches catalogued by academic archives at UCSB and the Miller Center.
The only editorial choice we’ve made is how to organize it. Eighteen recurring topics, assigned by a small machine-learning model to make long scrolls readable. Each topic has its own color. You can hide the ones you don’t care about and keep the ones you do.
No commentary. No framing. Just the record.
